new_classes.label_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol of an alcoholic strength by volume of 80% vol or higher (subheading 2207 10)
new_classes.label_de: Ethylalkohol mit einem Alkoholgehalt von 80 % vol oder mehr, unvergällt (Unterposition 2207 10)
new_classes.definition_en: Undenatured ethyl alcohol of an alcoholic strength by volume of 80% vol or higher, classified under CN subheading 2207 10. Excludes spirituous beverages (e.g. gin, vodka) which fall under subheadings 2208 20 12 to 2208 90 78 regardless of their alcoholic strength.

new_classes.label_en: Undenatured quality (ethyl alcohol not treated with denaturing agents)
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by ethyl alcohol indicating that it has not been denatured by addition of substances rendering it unfit for human consumption, distinguishing subheading 2207 10 (undenatured) from 2207 20 (denatured).
A quality borne by an ethyl alcohol product indicating that its alcoholic strength by volume is at least 80%, used to discriminate subheading 2207 10 from subheading 2208 (ethyl alcohol < 80% vol).

new_classes.label_en: Not-a-spirituous-beverage quality (excludes gin, vodka and other spirituous beverages of subheadings 2208 20 12–2208 90 78)
new_classes.label_de: Keine-Spirituose-Eigenschaft (kein Gin, Wodka oder sonstige Spirituosen der Unterpositionen 2208 20 12–2208 90 78)
new_classes.definition_en: A quality indicating that the ethyl alcohol product is not a spirituous beverage such as gin or vodka, which would fall under subheadings 2208 20 12 to 2208 90 78 regardless of alcoholic strength.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the three main distinguishing criteria of CN 2207 10: (1) the product is undenatured ethyl alcohol (UndenaturedQuality), (2) its alcoholic strength is at least 80% vol (AlcoholicStrengthAtLeast80VolQuality), and (3) it is not a spirituous beverage (gin, vodka, etc.) which would fall under subheadings 2208 20 12 to 2208 90 78 (NotSpiritousBeverageQuality + complement restriction against SpiritsBeverages2208). The exact ≥ 80% vol threshold is expressed as a named quality class rather than a numeric datatype restriction on alcoholByVolumePercent, because the TBox property alcoholByVolumePercent lacks a domain and the running TBox does not yet define a numeric threshold class analogous to SmallContainer/LargeContainer for alcohol strength. The coverage_score is reduced slightly (0.85) because the numeric threshold axiom (alcoholByVolumePercent ≥ 80.0) is expressed symbolically via a named quality class rather than as a full OWL datatype restriction, and because the HS Explanatory Note cross-reference (fourth paragraph exclusion) is of a purely textual/legal nature and cannot be captured in OWL axioms.
